fingerprints.errorPattern: Stripe webhook signature verification failed: No signatures found matching the expected signature
fingerprints.reversalPattern: Removed express.json() middleware before webhook route, used express.raw() instead
fingerprints.warning: Stripe webhook signature check will always fail if express.json() has already consumed the raw body.
fingerprints.fix: Place the webhook route BEFORE express.json() and use express.raw({ type: 'application/json' }) on that route only.

fingerprints.errorPattern: Clerk: auth() called but no ClerkProvider found in the component tree; session is always null
fingerprints.reversalPattern: Wrapped entire Next.js app with ClerkProvider in layout.tsx and moved middleware.ts to project root
fingerprints.warning: Clerk auth() always returns null when middleware.ts is missing from the project root or ClerkProvider is absent from layout.
fingerprints.fix: Add middleware.ts at the project root exporting clerkMiddleware(), and wrap <html> body in <ClerkProvider> inside app/layout.tsx.

fingerprints.errorPattern: Property '{field}' does not exist on type 'PrismaClient'; run prisma generate
fingerprints.reversalPattern: Ran npx prisma generate to regenerate client after schema.prisma change
fingerprints.warning: Prisma TypeScript types are stale — the generated client does not reflect the current schema.prisma.
fingerprints.fix: Run `npx prisma generate` after every schema change. Add it as a postinstall script: "postinstall": "prisma generate".

fingerprints.errorPattern: useRouter is not a function or returns undefined in Next.js App Router; imported from 'next/router'
fingerprints.reversalPattern: Changed import from 'next/router' to 'next/navigation' for App Router pages
fingerprints.warning: next/router's useRouter does not work in the Next.js 14 App Router — use useRouter from 'next/navigation' instead.
fingerprints.fix: Replace `import { useRouter } from 'next/router'` with `import { useRouter } from 'next/navigation'` in all App Router components.

fingerprints.errorPattern: Error [ERR_REQUIRE_ESM]: require() of ES Module {path} not supported; must use import()
fingerprints.reversalPattern: Switched project to ESM (type: module in package.json) or used dynamic import() for the module
fingerprints.warning: Package is pure ESM and cannot be loaded with require(). CJS callers must use dynamic import() or the project must switch to ESM.
fingerprints.fix: Add "type": "module" to package.json, rename .js files to .mjs, or use `const mod = await import("pkg")` at call sites.

fingerprints.errorPattern: Supabase query returns empty array or null despite rows existing; RLS policy denies all access
fingerprints.reversalPattern: Added SELECT RLS policy for authenticated role or used service_role key for server-side queries
fingerprints.warning: Supabase RLS is enabled but no policy exists for the role — all reads silently return empty.
fingerprints.fix: Add a SELECT policy: `CREATE POLICY "allow read" ON table FOR SELECT USING (true)`, or use the service_role key only on the server.

fingerprints.errorPattern: COPY failed: file not found in build context or excluded by .dockerignore: {path}
fingerprints.reversalPattern: Fixed Dockerfile COPY source path to be relative to build context, updated .dockerignore
fingerprints.warning: COPY paths in Dockerfile are relative to the build context (the directory passed to docker build), not the Dockerfile location.
fingerprints.fix: Run `docker build -f docker/Dockerfile .` from the repo root, and ensure .dockerignore does not exclude the files you need.

fingerprints.errorPattern: useEffect fires twice on mount in development causing duplicate API calls or double subscriptions
fingerprints.reversalPattern: Added cleanup function to useEffect and moved one-time init to useRef guard or outside component
fingerprints.warning: React 18 StrictMode intentionally mounts/unmounts/remounts every component in dev — useEffect runs twice to expose missing cleanup.
fingerprints.fix: Return a cleanup function from useEffect. For truly one-time side effects use a module-level variable or a ref: `const ran = useRef(false)`.

fingerprints.errorPattern: OpenAI streaming response hangs or delivers full response at once instead of streaming in Next.js API route
fingerprints.reversalPattern: Switched to Edge Runtime and used ReadableStream with Response instead of Node.js res.write()
fingerprints.warning: Node.js API routes in Next.js buffer the full response before sending — streaming requires the Edge Runtime or a proper ReadableStream.
fingerprints.fix: Add `export const runtime = 'edge'` to the route file and return `new Response(stream)` using the OpenAI SDK's `.toReadableStream()`.

fingerprints.errorPattern: JsonWebTokenError: invalid signature — JWT verify secret does not match signing secret
fingerprints.reversalPattern: Unified JWT_SECRET env var between signing and verifying services, confirmed no whitespace in env value
fingerprints.warning: JWT "invalid signature" almost always means the signing and verifying code use different secrets, including trailing newlines in env files.
fingerprints.fix: Use `process.env.JWT_SECRET?.trim()` when reading the secret and ensure the same variable is set in both services.

fingerprints.errorPattern: Prisma migrate dev detected drift: database schema is not in sync with migration history
fingerprints.reversalPattern: Ran prisma migrate reset in dev or created a new migration with prisma migrate dev --name fix-drift
fingerprints.warning: Prisma detected the database was modified outside of migrations — migrate dev will fail until drift is resolved.
fingerprints.fix: In dev run `npx prisma migrate reset`. In prod, create a baseline migration with `prisma migrate resolve --applied <migration_name>`.

fingerprints.errorPattern: CORS policy blocked: response to preflight OPTIONS request does not return HTTP 200 with Access-Control headers
fingerprints.reversalPattern: Added explicit OPTIONS handler returning 200 with Access-Control-Allow-Origin and Access-Control-Allow-Methods headers
fingerprints.warning: Browsers send an OPTIONS preflight before cross-origin POST/PUT/DELETE — if the server does not respond 200 with CORS headers the real request never fires.
fingerprints.fix: Handle OPTIONS explicitly: `if (req.method === 'OPTIONS') { res.setHeader('Access-Control-Allow-Origin','*'); res.sendStatus(200); }`

fingerprints.errorPattern: GitHub Actions step failed: Error: Resource not accessible by integration — missing 'write' permission in workflow
fingerprints.reversalPattern: Added permissions block to workflow YAML with contents: write or packages: write as required
fingerprints.warning: GitHub Actions defaults to read-only GITHUB_TOKEN since 2022 — steps that push, publish, or create releases require explicit 'write' permissions.
fingerprints.fix: Add a top-level permissions block to the workflow: `permissions:\n  contents: write\n  packages: write`

fingerprints.errorPattern: Tailwind CSS utility classes have no effect in browser; styles not generated in output CSS
fingerprints.reversalPattern: Added correct glob patterns to content array in tailwind.config.ts to match all component files
fingerprints.warning: Tailwind only generates CSS for classes found in files matched by the content array — classes in unmatched files are silently dropped.
fingerprints.fix: Set content: ['./src/**/*.{ts,tsx,js,jsx,html}'] in tailwind.config.ts. Restart the dev server after changing the config.

fingerprints.errorPattern: npm ERR! ERESOLVE unable to resolve dependency tree: peer dependency conflict between installed packages
fingerprints.reversalPattern: Used --legacy-peer-deps flag or manually aligned package versions to satisfy peer requirements
fingerprints.warning: npm v7+ enforces peer dependencies strictly — conflicting peer ranges block installation unless explicitly overridden.
fingerprints.fix: Run `npm install --legacy-peer-deps` as a short-term fix, then audit with `npm ls` and align versions in package.json using overrides/resolutions.

fingerprints.errorPattern: Invariant Violation: Native module {ModuleName} cannot be null; module not linked in Expo build
fingerprints.reversalPattern: Ran expo prebuild and rebuilt native binary after installing native module, switched from Expo Go to dev build
fingerprints.warning: Expo Go cannot load native modules that require custom native code — a development build is required.
fingerprints.fix: Run `npx expo prebuild` then `npx expo run:ios` / `npx expo run:android`. Use `eas build --profile development` for CI.

fingerprints.errorPattern: Redis connection timeout or ECONNREFUSED in serverless function; new client created on every invocation
fingerprints.reversalPattern: Moved Redis client creation outside handler to module scope and used Upstash REST client instead of tcp for serverless
fingerprints.warning: TCP Redis clients time out in serverless because each invocation creates a new connection that the short-lived function never closes.
fingerprints.fix: Use @upstash/redis (HTTP-based) for Vercel/Lambda, or declare the Redis client at module scope so it is reused across warm invocations.

fingerprints.errorPattern: tRPC: Type error — input/output types on client do not match server router; TypeScript sees stale AppRouter type
fingerprints.reversalPattern: Restarted TypeScript server to pick up updated AppRouter type export after server procedure signature changed
fingerprints.warning: After changing a tRPC procedure signature the TypeScript language server often caches the old AppRouter type — the error is misleading.
fingerprints.fix: Restart the TS server (VS Code: "TypeScript: Restart TS Server"). Ensure the client imports AppRouter type from the server file directly.

fingerprints.errorPattern: process.env.{VAR} is undefined at build time in Vercel despite being set in project environment variables
fingerprints.reversalPattern: Prefixed variable with NEXT_PUBLIC_ for client exposure or moved usage to runtime (getServerSideProps / Server Component)
fingerprints.warning: Vercel env vars without NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix are only available server-side at runtime, not inlined at build time.
fingerprints.fix: Prefix client-side vars with NEXT_PUBLIC_. For server-only vars used at build time, redeploy after setting them — they are not retroactively injected.

fingerprints.errorPattern: ERROR: type 'vector' does not exist — pgvector extension not enabled in PostgreSQL database
fingerprints.reversalPattern: Ran CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector in the target database before running migrations
fingerprints.warning: PostgreSQL does not ship with pgvector enabled — the 'vector' type is unavailable until the extension is explicitly created.
fingerprints.fix: Run `CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector;` in your database (or add it to your first migration). On Supabase it's available in the Dashboard under Extensions.
